ops.action: Exclude tool-specific project files (e.g., `.serena/*`, local IDE metadata, assistant-runner configs) from templates and committed scaffolds unless they are a deliberate product feature; prefer removing them rather than ignoring via template-local `.gitignore`.
ops.title: Do not ship editor/agent tool artifacts in templates
ops.trigger: When adding or reviewing files under template trees like src/agent_loom/compound/opencode/ (or other scaffolded outputs).

ops.action: Treat the installed `.opencode/` (and any `.loom/compound/` docs) as a contract: preserve stable paths, stable file ordering, stable newline formatting, and stable rendered content; immediately update/extend `tests/test_compound_install.py` to lock the behavior.
ops.title: Keep compound install outputs deterministic
ops.trigger: When changing compound scaffold/template contents or compound install logic (e.g., src/agent_loom/compound/scaffold.py, src/agent_loom/compound/paths.py, src/agent_loom/compound/docs.py, compound opencode template tree).

ops.action: Put workflow guidance in `.opencode/commands/workflow-*.md` (command-discoverable docs) and avoid duplicating full skill content inside the compound template unless required; keep skills procedural and source-of-truth in `.opencode/skills/` at repo root.
ops.title: Prefer workflow command docs over duplicating skills in templates
ops.trigger: When documenting workflows for end-users inside compound-installed `.opencode/` trees.

ops.action: Write links and references using repo-root-relative paths (no absolute machine paths, no editor URIs) and avoid references to removed/retired template files (e.g., AGENTS.md copies) to keep docs portable and deterministic.
ops.title: Use repo-root-relative paths in template docs
ops.trigger: When editing README/docs content that will be scaffolded into a repo (e.g., src/agent_loom/compound/opencode/README.md, src/agent_loom/compound/opencode/.loom/compound/README.md, `.opencode/commands/*.md`).
